assumptions: The v2.15 interacting graph-domain equality D(A0+V)=D(A0) holds.
The v2.14 termwise commutator classifications cover every complete-operator term.
limitations: This closes projector graph-domain invariance only; it does not close lower-bound transfer or index/mirror theorem gates.

rows.domain_action: P_perp commutes with diagonal A0 on D(A0)
evidence: A0 is diagonal in the formal kernel/complement split.

rows.domain_action: Hopf term preserves sector-labeled domain blocks
evidence: v2.14 classifies [P_perp,V_Hopf] as zero by sector.
v2.14 classifies [P_perp,V_boundary] as zero by sector.

rows.domain_action: Chirality term preserves protected chirality split
evidence: v2.14 classifies [P_perp,V_chi] as zero by chirality.
v2.14 gives a_C = 0.015621013485509948 for the nonzero sector-coupling commutator.

rows.domain_action: Lift is supported on the complement projector
evidence: P_perp_lift is constructed from P_perp support.

rows.domain_action: PSD profile term is bounded/lower-bound safe on the represented profile sector
evidence: D(A0+V)=D(A0) and graph norms are equivalent in the v2.15 interacting-domain audit.

rows.domain_action: Represented topographic sector maps into lifted/screened complement channels
evidence: v2.11/v2.12 represent mixed and bundle-curvature channels by topographic lift/screening sectors.

rows.domain_action: Curvature/topographic remainder is represented by lift/profile support
evidence: v2.13 action uniqueness forbids an independent unrepresented curvature term.

title: BHSM v2.15 Projector Domain-Invariance Report
